name: The Coral
text: We did the video in the local alleyways and social clubs. There were belly dancers, ballroom dancers, monks gowns and an Alsatian called Charlie. It was a bit like The Rolling Thunder Review, but in a retirement town.

name: BBC Springwatch
text: 10 days to go! #Winterwatch January 19 – 22 BBC2 Hoping for red squirrels, pic by 2slo7 http://t.co/H2zpBDHU9m http://t.co/pU6EPMyvxd

name: The Coral
text: She was a desert artist, and danced around with old ram’s skulls in the video. Later she came out with a guitar and Sang Hank Williams’ Long Gone Lonesome Blues, with all the yodelling parts.

name: The Coral
text: We knocked at this old woman’s shack and asked if we could film inside. Her name was Jenny Olsen. She let us have the run of the place, no questions asked.

name: The Coral
text: We told Alan Wills, and he booked @thewildoutdoors on a plane with a super8 camera the next day. That was that, we were doing a video in the Wild West. It was a fun, impulsive time.

name: The Coral
text: For the video, we were passing through Death Valley on tour with Supergrass. We decided to stop do a video in a town called Randsburg, an old cowboy ghost town with a small jail and a saloon bar.

name: The Coral
text: We went to a forest near where we live to shoot the video. We’d not long come back from America so we had this kind of gypsy look that we’d picked up along the way. All of our colours seemed to match up with the music, the album cover, the videos. We got everything spot on.

name: James Skelly
text:
@thecoralband our mate Joe Fearon used to say ‘it’s a damp dog day’ when it was raining. That’s where the title comes from. 

Most of the lyrics came from a book I had about pirates. I was obsessed with ‘Your Mind and we Belong Together by Love. I was trying to write a tune like that.

name: The Coral
text: A.D.D. Stands for A Damp Dog Day, which is like a crappy, bad weather day. It was a cold winter. Joe Fearon was at the session in @ElevatorStudios. Joe set up Deltasonic with Alan. They were best mates.

name: The Coral
text: Reminds me of ‘I Won’t Hurt You’ by the West Coast Pop Art Band.

name: The Coral
text: He was always recommending us albums and movies. I think ADD had some connection to the Dog Day Afternoon film. Joe had loads of sayings like this. We were all standing around saying, who shall it be the confessions of? And he said A DAMP DOG DAY straight off. So it stuck.

name: James Skelly
text:
@thecoralband I was trying to write a song Jonny Mathis could sing. It’s a bit throw away really but listening back it’s better than I remembered. 

The jam at the end is great I think Bill came up with that.

name: The Coral
text: I wanted to write a song like ‘Rockin’ Chair’ by The Band or ‘I’m Only Sleeping’ by The Beatles. Like Secret Kiss, this has a funeral procession feel: death in Toon-town, the knee-bones-connected-to-the-thigh-bone.

name: James Skelly
text:
@thecoralband I think we had this one before the first album. Bill and I demoed it in his bedroom. Ian said it sounded like Travis so we shelved it 🤷‍♂️

When we came toward the end of the recording, Alan Wills said, “You haven’t got any singles”, so we revisited this and Pass It On.

name: The Coral
text: Alan Wills read from ‘Under Milk Wood’ here, which was a big influence on the album. Alan lived in Wales as a kid and loved the book, so it was a great fit. “Sunny Slow.”

name: The Coral
text: This is what it’s like walking around a small town on acid, laughing at the lifeboats, Ian’s hands turning to sausages, Barry from Eastenders in the chippy, the roof of every house laughing at you. All the alleyways and secret stairwells and shops unchanged in a hundred years.

name: James Skelly
text:
@thecoralband The Walker Brothers would’ve done a great version of this. I stole a lyric off ‘The Soft Sands’ by The Chordettes for the second verse. 

The video for secret kiss is possibly my favourite Coral video. #TimsTwitterListeningParty @Tim_Burgess

name: The Coral
text: One of our more magical songs. Could have been written in 1906 or 2040. A song out of time and space. We listened to a lot of Chordettes, Love, eerie ballroom music.

name: James Skelly
text: @thecoralband 🤣I was on holiday in Portugal with my girlfriend and we went to this gypsy market where I bought some boots. When we got back to the apartment I had a spliff and wrote it on a little guitar. Not the best song ever but the energy of the band carries it #TimsTwitterListeningParty

name: James Skelly
text:
.@thecoralband we were on the tour bus somewhere and Bill tought me how to play a Simon and Garfunkel tune on the guitar. Next minute me and Nick were writing Leizah. 

The character was based on a character from ‘The Sun Also Rises’ by Earnest Hemingway.

name: The Coral
text: It’s definitely got a Cheech & Chong feel. Reminds me of ‘Motorpsycho Nightmare’ by Bob Dylan.

name: Paul Duffy💀
text: @thecoralband Influenced by a Nat King Cole tune I seem to remember

name: The Coral
text: Such an effortless tune. We were having a good time. It was written on the way to a gig in Birmingham Institute. We learned that clawhammer picking style and thought, “We’ll get so many fucking songs out of this.” (see ‘After The Fair’, ‘Fallin All Around You’, ‘Cobwebs’).

name: James Skelly
text: @thecoralband Could be my favourite @thecoralband track. I remember looking at the moon after we played @glastonbury when I was tripping. I thought I’d invented a new chord and Ian said, “Sounds shit to me” and that’s where the title came from. #TimsTwitterListeningParty

name: Paul Duffy💀
text: @thecoralband Got well into the beach boys for the bass line

name: James Skelly
text: @thecoralband The idea for the chorus lyrics came from a woody Guthrie song and we worked backwards from there. #TimsTwitterListeningParty

name: James Skelly
text:
@thecoralband wrote this on the train in my head without a guitar when I was about 17 that’s why the melody is so pure. 
When we recorded the version for the album Lee had tendinitis so @IanZBroudie played rhythm guitar it has a great feel I love how subtle Bills guitar playing is on this

name: The Coral
text: After being home for a while, we decided the next album should be about where we lived; the dark side of small town life. Street parties, silver tea-sets, secret suicides. All the different characters: Leizah, Bill McCai, Captain Cat, ADDD. Under Milk Wood.

name: The Coral
text: We recorded Magic and Medicine with @ianzbroudie in the Christmas period of 2002 when the debut hadn’t been out for four months. By January I think we had 35 songs tracked. Then we went straight back out to America and Japan.

text: https://t.co/p4TF6EE3ik Official statement from Faith Cheltenham, ex. Thank you to @RealWalkAway and Mr. @BrandonStraka and Libby and everyone at #walkaway who have inspired me as BiNet USA President to announce my conservative status and my vote for @realDonaldTrump.

name: BiNet USA
text:
About the bisexual pride flag...
A statement prepared by the board of BiNet USA.

 MYTH: The Bisexual Pride flag is open for use by everyone for any purpose, including the confederate flag. 

FULL STATEMENT: https://t.co/sNsZUcckop

name: Lauren B. Beach, JD/PhD
text: Update: A statement about the bisexual pride flag was posted on the #BiNet USA blog by Faith Cheltenham (link below). I am unsure if any additional statement will be forthcoming. I have resigned from the BiNet board. https://t.co/xb0KjnWa9s

name: Lauren B. Beach, JD/PhD
text: I want to clarify these are the views of #BiNet President @thefayth, who also was the person tweeting this content earlier today from the now-defunct BiNet twitter. I do not share the view that #BiNet owns or should seek to license the #bisexual pride flag.

name: Lauren B. Beach, JD/PhD
text: @northofnothing_ @DrGBeth The board has convened an emergency meeting today and will issue a statement after the meeting ASAP.

name: BiNet USA
text: The copyright of the flag is solely BiNet USA’s; it does not belong to any one founder and no founder can approve its use without the President and/or board approval. And yet @Target where can we send a letter? Cause you sell #biprideflags too? Where is our cut?

Last week, I lost my grandmother.

Her name was Winell Thomas and we called her Mama Win. 

She was born in Cuba, and immigrated to this country from Jamaica. She taught public school for nearly 40 years. She came here to build a life, and she ended up building a legacy.

name: The Charlatans
text:
The album cover is Pickmere Lake, Cheshire. Just outside of Northwich. 

#TimsTwitterListeningParty

name: Tim Burgess
text:
Apples and Oranges 
I was not a happy lad when I wrote the words for this but I think the words are decent #TimsTwitterListeningParty

name: Tim Burgess
text:
Try Again Today

Written in LA with Linus of Hollywood who co wrote my album I Believe. 
We recorded it and re-recorded it so many times trying to get the vibe of the demo 
Ken Nelson got the best out of us as did @jimspencermusic (as always) #TimsTwitterListeningParty

name: The Charlatans
text:
Track 10 - Loving You Is Easy

I was with @TonyCharlatan before he left Walsall for Ireland. Tony insisted on the vocal swap halfway trough. Love the lyrics on this. We were demo-ing and Tony just started jamming at the end. He wanted it cutting. We insisted it stayed.

name: Tony Rogers
text: Minnie Ripperton has a song by the same title,  #TimsTwitterListeningParty

name: Tim Burgess
text:
It was hard trying to keep the band and my marriage together - think this song reveals that to me now. 
So much travelling, so much drinking, so many time differences. Saying that, this is one of my favourite songs on the album #TimsTwitterListeningParty

name: Tim Burgess
text:
I’ll Sing A Hymn (You Came To Me) 
I am not really sure what I think of this one. 
I was channelling Please Don’t Leave Me Lonely by King Floyd but I am not sure if it strikes the chords I wanted to strike #TimsTwitterListeningParty

name: Tim Burgess
text:
Blue For you
Written about Rob. Cathartic? Yes, 
but did it need to go on the album? Let’s see #TimsTwitterListeningParty

name: Tony Rogers
text:
Cry yourself to sleep, 
Bit of a sad song really, Daughters emigrated to Australia, they used to cry a lot cuz they didn’t want to leave.  #TimsTwitterListeningParty

name: Tim Burgess
text:
Cry Yourself To Sleep
Tony had a ton of songs this was one of them. Melody and words. 
I might have added a few words - but I don’t think I did 
Tony should maybe have sung this one, like Loving You Is Easy #TimsTwitterListeningParty

name: Tim Burgess
text: I was very inspired it seems by When I Paint My Masterpiece by Bob Dylan #TimsTwitterListeningParty

name: Tim Burgess
text:
As I Watch You in Disbelief

It’s a story of being broken is it boigraphical? Yes #TimsTwitterListeningParty

name: The Charlatans
text:
Track 10 - Try Again Today

Great song. Always thought the finish on this version is slightly overproduced. A chap called Mike Cave helped mix the first version. That version is on Forever: The Singles (the expanded version)

#TimsTwitterListeningParty

name: The Charlatans
text:
Track 2 - Feel The Pressure

Mark came up with the original music. The Chemical Brothers did their thing to it. The original band version is probably more in-keeping with the album. Both are great.

#TimsTwitterListeningParty

name: Tim Burgess
text:
Feel the pressure

Full horror movie intro provided by our long time collaborators @ChemBros #TimsTwitterListeningParty

name: Tim Burgess
text:
Up At The Lake 

Title came from a film by David Lynch that never got made. I thought we could at least provide the soundtrack to this unmade film #TimsTwitterListeningParty

name: Millarworld

Two all time lows were beaten on 24 January:
-8.3C Tobishima and -11.6C at Matsumae + 2 more with short POR
On 25 January Ushibuka tied its all time low with -3.4C (POR 1949-)
The famous Mt. Fuji (3772m) with -36.1C,had its lowest temperature since 1997

Binance has never used BNB for collateral, and we have never taken on debt.
